title: Marine Le Pen decides to run for president after appeals convition
subhead: The far-right leader said she would appeal to France's highest court after her embezzlement conviction was upheld but her ban on holding elected office was shortened, allowing her to stand in the 2027 election.

title: US launches new strikes on Iran after Hormuz attacks
On the first day of the NATO summit in Ankara, Rutte unveils a wave of defense contracts worth billions of euros
Two Israeli NGOs warn of a 'de facto annexation at an unprecedented pace' in the West Bank
subhead: Israel now controls 18% of the occupied territories, compared to 7% before the war in Gaza. A report from the non-profit organizations Peace Now and Kerem Navot has been documenting the acceleration of land grabs, the construction of settler-only roads and the expulsion of local communities over the past three and a half years.

title: In Venezuela, hope of finding survivors after the twin earthquakes gives way to mourning
subhead: After the twin earthquakes of June 24, the chances of finding anyone alive beneath the rubble are now extremely slim. Amid the devastated landscape, Venezuelan photographer Alejandro Cegarra documented for Le Monde the search efforts taking place in the ruins and how Venezuelan society has organized itself in the wake of the disaster.

title: In Quebec, 'moving day' overshadowed by housing crisis
subhead: A unique tradition in the Canadian province, tens of thousands of people move on the same day, July 1. But in recent years, this ritual has been marred by serious difficulties in finding new housing.

title: Book reveals Trump's improvisation and concentration of power
subhead: Two journalists from the 'New York Times' retrace the early months of the US president's second term, highlighting the ruptures and upheavals his approach to power has unleashed.

PFAS: Less than 2% of emissions could be removed from environment, even with massive investments
subhead: A study by scientists and journalists from the Forever Pollution Project, coordinated by Le Monde, reveals that cleanup efforts are largely ineffective and highlights the urgent need for strict limits on emissions.

title: 250 years of American independence: How the text that founded the US – and changed the world – was drafted
subhead: 'America 250' (1/13). The 13 colonies had been fighting the British Crown for a year when, in July 1776, lawyer Thomas Jefferson completed the drafting of the Declaration of Independence. Adopted by Congress on July 4 in Philadelphia, this document, which established the principle of equality for all – even though slavery was still legal everywhere – founded the United States of America.

title: Netanyahu government's legal coup challenges Israel's justice system: 'The final nail in the coffin of democracy'
subhead: By declaring he would not comply with a Supreme Court ruling, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is directly confronting the country's highest legal authority, plunging Israel into a constitutional crisis.
